name: Okojo
tagline: ECMAScript engine and flexible host container for .NET
summary: Okojo aims for browser-grade JavaScript correctness while keeping the core engine separate from browser, Node, and hosting policy.

sections.title: Core
items: Parser, compiler, bytecode VM, realms, agents, modules
Minimal public low-level host seams for embedders
No Node or browser policy in the core assembly

sections.title: Hosting
items: Manual and thread-affine host event loops
Execution budgets and app/game style host control

sections.title: Profiles
items: Okojo.WebPlatform for portable Web APIs and Okojo.Browser for browser-oriented APIs
Okojo.Node for CommonJS, ESM, and selected built-ins
Focused sandboxes for browser, game loop, Node, and CLI usage

nextSteps: Keep pushing practical Node package compatibility
Use bytecode and real package probes to drive compiler fixes
Stabilize the embedder-facing API split across assemblies
